title: camellia caudata var. gracilis
intro: Camellia caudata var. gracilis, also known as the 'Yunnan Tea Camellia', is a plant species belonging to the Camellia genus. It is a type of evergreen shrub native to the Yunnan Province in China, traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The leaves of Camellia caudata var. gracilis have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, contributing to its health benefits such as reducing blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, with the most common form being a tea known as 'Yunnan Tea', which is also used as a digestive aid and to promote relaxation.
